memory_content: The project uses PostgreSQL with connection pooling via pgBouncer.
CI runs lint, typecheck, and tests on every pull request.
Production deployments happen through GitHub Actions with manual approval.
Feature flags are stored in Redis and cached for thirty seconds.
The API gateway enforces rate limits of 120 requests per minute per token.
Session data is signed and stored in HTTP-only secure cookies.
The frontend uses React Query for server-state caching.
Authentication uses OAuth with Google and GitHub providers.
The service emits OpenTelemetry traces to the collector endpoint.
Logs are structured JSON lines with request_id and user_id fields.
The mobile app supports offline drafts synchronized on reconnect.
User avatars are resized and optimized by an image worker service.
Email notifications are queued in RabbitMQ before delivery.
The billing system reconciles Stripe webhooks every night.
API responses include ETag headers for cache validation.
A nightly job compacts old audit records into monthly partitions.
Secrets are loaded from environment variables, never committed to git.
The default API timeout is fifteen seconds with one retry.
Customer support tags urgent tickets with the label P1.
The search index is rebuilt when schema version changes.
Staging uses a smaller instance size than production.
The docs site is generated from Markdown using Astro.
Developers run pre-commit hooks for formatting and lint checks.
The app exposes health and readiness probes at slash healthz and slash readyz.
Backups are encrypted with AES-256 and stored for thirty days.
The analytics pipeline batches events every sixty seconds.
The design system uses semantic color tokens for themes.
User uploads are scanned for malware before being persisted.
The team follows trunk-based development with short-lived branches.
SLO targets 99.9 percent uptime for the public API.
Customer data exports are generated asynchronously and emailed as links.
The app supports dark mode and follows system preference by default.
The team tracks incidents in PagerDuty with postmortem templates.
GraphQL is used internally while public APIs remain REST.
The application caches product catalogs in Redis for five minutes.
A cron task purges expired sessions every hour.
The QA checklist requires accessibility checks for keyboard navigation.
New accounts must verify email before creating API tokens.
The team uses semantic versioning for all public package releases.
Frontend bundles are analyzed weekly to monitor size regressions.
The localization pipeline manages translations in Crowdin.
Password reset links expire after twenty minutes.
The desktop client auto-updates from signed release artifacts.
Data migrations run inside transactional boundaries whenever possible.
The API uses idempotency keys for payment creation endpoints.
Search suggestions are generated from popular queries over the last seven days.
The observability stack includes Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboards.
The support bot escalates to human agents after two failed answers.
Feature discovery tours run only for first-time users.
The team uses canary releases with automatic rollback on error spikes.
Function `buildSearchIndex()` precomputes trigram tokens before inserting documents.
query: where are trigrams built for indexing

Error message `SQLITE_BUSY: database is locked` was fixed by setting `PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000`.
Method `MemoryService.recallForSession(sessionId)` merges recent prompts into one semantic query.
JavaScript call site uses `new AbortController()` to cancel long-running embedding requests.
